column1: a11abb0e-7829-4a92-8c4d-8908cda2e2c2,342896289,Deloitte,"Manager/Senior Manager, AI Governance, Risk and Data",on_site,"Ontario, Canada",2024-09-20T16:35:02.040Z,"

Job Type: Permanent
Work Model: Hybrid
Reference code: 126325
Primary Location: Toronto, ON
All Available Locations: Toronto, ON

At Deloitte, we are driven to inspire and help our people, organization, communities, and country to thrive. Our Purpose is to build a better future by accelerating and expanding access to knowledge. Purpose defines who we are and gives us reason to exist as an organization.
By living our Purpose, we will make an impact that matters.

• Have many careers in one Firm.
  • Partner with clients to solve their most complex problems
  • Enjoy flexible, proactive, and practical benefits that foster a culture of well-being and connectedness.

column4: >What will your typical day look like?

column1: Our mission is to use data and analytics to deliver value for our clients. Our team understands exactly how data underpins a business and how improving the management, use and insight from this data improves performance and reduces risk. We also help clients manage the new risks and safely leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning. For this role, we are seeking Consultant or Senior Consultant who will help deliver on our ambitious plans to grow our practice.

We are seeking an experienced professional with an experience in Data and AI, with a strong focus on responsible AI, data & AI risk management, and regulatory compliance. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of generating and managing a portfolio of AI enablement services. This role includes developing and nurturing client relationships, leading project teams, coaching and mentoring professionals, and maintaining a continuous focus on personal and professional growth. The role will have full life-cycle responsibility over Responsible AI, strategy, and AI Risk, including identifying opportunities, proposal development, project delivery, team leadership, and ensuring the quality of project deliverables.
The successful candidate will have responsibility to:

• Develop and execute comprehensive AI strategies that align with clients' goals, mission, and vision. Embed risk management frameworks into the AI lifecycle to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
  • Support our clients to grow responsibly through the use of applied data and analytics.
  • Contribute to the development of thought leadership in the field of responsible AI, data & AI risk management, and regulatory compliance. Support the industry leadership in growing our AI governance and risk analytics business.
  • Stay abreast of industry trends, technology innovations, and AI regulatory requirements. Ensure all AI solutions comply with relevant laws and regulations.
  • Support the establishment of a repeatable analytical models and approaches for clients.
  • Identify opportunities for new business, develop proposals, and assist in pursuit efforts to secure new engagements. Work with other Deloitte functions to further the use of data & analytics in go to market activities.
  • Coach and mentor team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional growth. Provide guidance and support to help team members in delivery and achieve their career goals.
  • Ensure the highest levels of quality in project deliverables, maintaining the standards expected of a leading professional services firm.
  • Have a clear understanding of the firm's commitment to creating a more inclusive culture.

We are the one of the largest Risk Advisory practices in Canada. Our Data and Analytics team is a key capability within Risk Advisory. We are a growing team with initiatives across multiple industries and use cases. We support organizations with all aspects of data and analytics related to risk management. We aim to bring recognized value to each engagement
column2: be it setting up a program to deliver on long term strategic objectives or solutioning for tactical needs under demanding timelines. We have extensive experience that we draw upon daily and are always looking to the future and innovative solutions and opportunities.

column4: >Enough about us, let’s talk about you

column1: In this role, the expected qualifications are:

• Bachelor's degree, or MS degree in Business, Economics, Technology Entrepreneurship, Computer Science, Data Science, or Machine Learning.
  • Minimum 6 years (Manager) / 8 years (Senior Manager) of experience of managing, strengthening, and broadening key client relationships, people and management skills.
  • Minimum 6 years (Manager) / 8 years (Senior Manager) of professional experience in data and AI, with a primary focus on responsible AI, data & AI risk management, and regulatory compliance.
  • Exceptional communication, presentation, business writing and professional skills, including being able to liaise with both technical and business contacts at our clients.
  • Superior written and verbal communication skills, including presentation and facilitation skills, and strong attention to detail.
  • Structured problem solving skills and logical thinking, as well as ability to develop and present new ideas and conceptualize new approaches and solutions.
  • Proven experience in AI ethics, responsible AI, or related fields, with a strong understanding of AI technologies and their ethical implications.
  • Strong data science and/or data engineering experience. Strong knowledge of AI technologies and frameworks.
  • Ability to stay updated with industry trends and AI regulatory requirements, and quickly adapt to new technologies.
  • Team player with the ability to work in a hybrid model (on-site and remote).

The salary range for this position is $97,000 - $231,000, and individuals may be eligible to participate in our bonus program. Deloitte is fair and competitive when it comes to the salaries of our people. We regularly benchmark across a variety of positions, industries, sectors, targets, and levels. Our approach is grounded on recognizing people's unique strengths and contributions and rewarding the value that they deliver.

Our Total Rewards Package extends well beyond traditional compensation and benefit programs and is designed to recognize employee contributions, encourage personal wellness, and support firm growth. Along with a competitive base salary and variable pay opportunities, we offer a wide array of initiatives that differentiate us as a people-first organization. Some representative examples include: $4,000 per year for mental health support benefits, a $1,300 flexible benefit spending account, 38+ days off (including 10 firm-wide closures known as ""Deloitte Days""), flexible work arrangements and a hybrid work structure.

column2: >Our promise to our people: Deloitte is where potential comes to life.

column1: We are a group of talented people who want to learn, gain experience, and develop skills. Wherever you are in your career, we want you to advance.

Diverse perspectives and life experiences make us better. Whoever you are and wherever you’re from, we want you to feel like you belong here. We provide flexible working options to support you and how you can contribute. Be the leader you want to be.
Be the leader you want to be
Some guide teams, some change culture, some build essential expertise. We offer opportunities and experiences that support your continuing growth as a leader.
Have as many careers as you want.

We are uniquely able to offer you new challenges and roles – and prepare you for them. We bring together people with unique experiences and talents, and we are the place to develop a lasting network of friends, peers, and mentors. Our TVP is about relationships – between leaders and their people, the firm and its people, peers, and within in our communities.

At Deloitte, we are all about doing business inclusively – that starts with having diverse colleagues of all abilities. Job Type: Permanent
Work Model: Hybrid
Reference code: 126337
Primary Location: Toronto, ON
All Available Locations: Toronto, ON

At Deloitte, we are driven to inspire and help our people, organization, communities, and country to thrive. Our Purpose is to build a better future by accelerating and expanding access to knowledge. Purpose defines who we are and gives us reason to exist as an organization.
By living our Purpose, we will make an impact that matters.

• Have many careers in one Firm.
  • Partner with clients to solve their most complex problems
  • Experience a firm where wellness matters.

column4: >What will your typical day look like?

column1: Our mission is to use data and analytics to deliver value for our clients. Our team understands exactly how data underpins a business and how improving the management, use and insight from this data improves performance and reduces risk. We also help clients manage the new risks and safely leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning. For this role, we are seeking Manager and Senior Manager level team members who will help deliver on our ambitious plans to grow our practice by providing leadership and technical / domain expertise.

As a Manager or Senior Manager, you will be leading engagement and client teams across data and analytics domains, helping some of the largest, most interesting organizations in the world imagine, deliver and run the future - transforming complex business problems and requirements into practical, implementable solutions. You will grow and mentor junior team members, and contribute to sales.

We combine our people's skills and experience, with Deloitte tools and solutions, across four focus capability areas across the end-to-end Risk Spectrum.
• Risk Data Strategy & Architecture
• Risk Data Modelling & Engineering
• Risk Data Analytics, Reporting & Insights
• Risk Data Governance & Management

The successful candidate will have responsibility to:
• Support our clients to grow responsibly through the use of applied data and analytics.
• Support the industry leadership in growing our risk analytics business.
• Support the establishment of a repeatable analytical models and approaches for clients.
• Work with other Deloitte functions to further the use of data & analytics in go to market activities.
• Manage and support team members in the delivery of work
• Build strong relationships with key Deloitte team members and clients.
• Enhance the value of our deliverables and the insights provided by our work.
• Support in reviews of data and date use for regulatory compliance and process and control effectiveness
• Work with existing clients to design and develop programs for data and analytics.
• Support the senior management team with go to market activities relating to risk and regulatory data and analytics projects.
• Have a clear understanding of the firm's commitment to creating a more inclusive culture.

We are the one of the largest Risk and Regulatory practices in Canada. Our Data and Analytics team is a key capability within the practice. We are a growing team with initiatives across multiple industries and use cases. We support organizations with all aspects of data and analytics related to risk management. We aim to bring recognized value to each engagement
column2: be it setting up a program to deliver on long term strategic objectives or solutioning for tactical needs under demanding timelines. We have extensive experience that we draw upon daily and are always looking to the future and innovative solutions and opportunities.

column4: >Enough about us, let’s talk about you

column1:
In this role, the expected qualifications are:

• Exceptional planning, organization, project and people management skills.
• Exceptional communication, presentation, business writing and professional skills, including being able to liaise with both technical and business contacts at our clients.
• Minimum 5 years (Manager) / 8 years (Senior Manager) of experience of managing, strengthening, and broadening key client relationships.
• Minimum 5 years (Manager) / 8 years (Senior Manager) of experience of managing and leading team delivery
• Experience in Financial Services Industry in a consulting or industry capacity
• Experience in Risk and Regulatory Compliance areas in a consulting or industry capacity
• Experience in delivering a broad range of data analytics engagements in areas such as, Data Strategy, Data Architecture, Data Migration, Data Analytics, Data Governance and Data Quality.
• Experience in applying Data Analytics, ML/AI and/or other insights approaches to deliver business value
• Experience in working with analytical, visualization and data engineering capabilities
• Experience in working with cloud capabilities is an asset
• Ability to own and drive initiatives in many different and challenging environments.
• Ability to understand client needs and deliver value through proven and innovative approaches.
• Ability to synthesize large amounts of information to produce clear, concise, and logical presentations and reports
• Ability to work in a fast paced and demanding environment, both independently and in a team setting
• Ability to establish priorities and meet challenging deadlines
• Curiosity and comfortability with ambiguity
• Undergraduate or Graduate Degree in relevant field
column2:
relevant professional qualification is an asset

column1:
The salary ranges for these roles depend on level and are between $84,000 - $231,000, and individuals may be eligible to participate in our bonus program. Deloitte is fair and competitive when it comes to the salaries of our people. We regularly benchmark across a variety of positions, industries, sectors, targets, and levels. Our approach is grounded on recognizing people's unique strengths and contributions and rewarding the value that they deliver.

Our Total Rewards Package extends well beyond traditional compensation and benefit programs and is designed to recognize employee contributions, encourage personal wellness, and support firm growth. Along with a competitive base salary and variable pay opportunities, we offer a wide array of initiatives that differentiate us as a people-first organization. Some representative examples include: $4,000 per year for mental health support benefits, a $1,300 flexible benefit spending account, 38+ days off (including 10 firm-wide closures known as ""Deloitte Days""), flexible work arrangements and a hybrid work structure.

Be the leader you want to be

column4: >Join our team and what we\'ll accomplish together

column2: >The AI Accelerator team is on a continuous journey towards helping TELUS become a world-class leader in data solutions, doing so by delivering data analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI solutions, high-quality data, and a data-product-oriented culture while always keeping an eye on the horizon, preparing for the next big thing.

>We are entrepreneurial and live by our AI Manifesto of failing fast and being outcome vs technology-driven, creating value for our customers, team members, communities, and the environment. The team takes pride in our Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning capabilities and takes ownership of each step of the process. From hypothesis generation, initial exploring of datasets, developing novel AI techniques to discover insights, to developing automation pipelines and web visualizations, we do it all!
>Always wanted to work with a team of innovators touching all business units within TELUS, and be part of a culture that embraces creativity and collaboration? If so, we’d love to talk with you!

>You’ll be a part of the team and journey that will transform the way we do business across various domains. You’ll collaborate with teams across the company, seeking out various data sources to help identify new business opportunities while championing data-driven decision-making and the accelerated adoption of AI.

>As a Senior Data Analyst on the team, you will combine your expert knowledge of data analytics and visualization with business acumen to create reporting tools to facilitate data exploration, analytics, to enable data-driven decision making for business stakeholders.
>Tackle complex data analysis challenges, working with big data to uncover key insights that drive business outcomes.
>Write SQL code involving multiple tables and aggregations, ensuring optimal query performance.

>Design and develop robust data models that effectively organize and structure data to support complex queries, reporting, and analytics. Ensure models are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with business requirements.

>Lead the development of visually compelling, interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Looker, DOMO, and Tableau. Transform raw data into easy-to-understand insights that directly impact decision-making. Create user-friendly data visualizations that communicate complex information effectively to a diverse audience, from executives to operational teams.
>Engineer end-to-end data solutions primarily on GCP services like BigQuery, GCS, Cloud Composer, and Looker, ensuring seamless integration and performance.
>Engage with various business stakeholders to gather requirements, develop dashboards and reports, and ensure the delivery of actionable insights.
>Lead a team of developers towards the implementation of operational and partnership dashboards using Looker, to help the leadership make informed data-driven decisions
>6+ years in roles such as Data Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, Data Engineer, or similar, with a strong focus on SQL and data modeling

>Expertise in creating and managing interactive dashboards and reports using leading BI tools such as Looker, DOMO, and Tableau. Ability to transform data into actionable insights for business stakeholders
>Strong experience with relational database systems, particularly BigQuery, Oracle, and SQL Server. Demonstrated ability to write complex SQL queries and optimize database performance
>Strong ability to identify requirements and build reporting solutions in collaboration with stakeholders
>Exceptional attention to detail, with the ability to prioritize, multi-task, and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment
>Proven track record of driving data-based decisions in a consultative style with executive and senior management stakeholders
>Post-secondary degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
>5 years of experience in the telecom industry
>Certifications in Data Engineering or Business Intelligence technologies

column1: 1a3e923e-f822-4d8f-a132-bd213da64fe8,343307867,Magna International,Senior Data Scientist,on_site,"Aurora, ONTARIO, CA, L4G 0G9",2024-09-23T16:41:02.184Z,"<div>

The Senior Data Scientist will be a key member of the data analytics team. In this role, you will use your expertise to solve data related challenges in manufacturing operations and vehicle data analysis. You have a strong sense of curiosity, along with an open mindset. Challenging the status quo, you continuously strive to learn and reach for new and innovative ideas. We encourage all individuals with an inventive mind who enjoy working with others to apply, we’d love to hear from you.
· Lead the data projects, apply, and inspire the adoption of advanced data science, analytics, and computer vision across the team and projects.

· Develop strategy and applications of statistical analysis, Machine Learning, Computer vision, and AI tools to improve product development, manufacturing, predictive maintenance, and business processes.
· Support, plan, and execute the initial and wider deployment and adoption of ML and AI initiatives, PoC, and small-scale implementation.
· Conceptualize Machine Learning problems, apply appropriate theory, explore approaches, execute simulations, and implement with minimal supervision.
· Leverage internal and external expertise as required.
· Regularly interface with senior management to provide project updates.
· Occasionally responsible to hire, lead, supervise, and evaluate indirect hires, such as summer students.

· Master’s Degree in Data Analytics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a closely related education with 7+ years of experience in industry with a focus on data analytics and/or computer vision.

· Alternate: Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a closely related education with 10+ years of experience in industry with a focus on data analytics and/or computer vision.
· Experience with a wide variety of ML and AI techniques and algorithms
· Experience with computer vision, structured light based 3D reconstruction of point cloud or mesh, feature estimation
· Experience with optimization algorithms for processing time and memory, parallel processing and implementation / Cuda
· Experience with agile project management and related tools such as Confluence and JIRA
· Experience with cloud-based tools by Microsoft, AWS, Databricks
· Analytic tools and languages for statistical and machine learning models, especially Python
· Deep Learning frameworks and tools such as TensorFlow
· Data management using PySpark and SQL

column1: ed080cfb-b859-4cca-82a7-b90d473ccaac,346257790,Sun Life Financial,"Director, Senior Data Analyst, Office of Chief Investment Officer - SLC Management",on_site,"Ontario, Canada",2024-10-14T08:50:01.993Z,"<div class=""css-1irs9pi"">

SLC Management is a growing global asset management firm providing innovative and diversified investment solutions to institutional investors. We are breaking new ground with a full spectrum of solutions for a growing institutional client base of more than 1300 organizations. Our history of organic and strategic growth reflects this commitment with over CA$374/US$283 billion in assets under management (AUM). As a subsidiary of Sun Life, we are aligned with one of the world’s leading financial services companies, and benefit from the stability and strength of that relationship.

We are a performance-driven organization with a deliberate focus on fostering a development-focused, inclusive culture revolving around our Core Values of being client obsessed, valuing each other, acting with speed and having an owner’s mindset. As part of our team, you play a role in fulfilling our purpose and making a difference. We offer a rewarding work environment that is filled with opportunity for growth where you will be empowered to meet the challenges of a highly competitive landscape.
Visit our website to learn more and for the most up to date AUM information.

SLC Management offers a mix of in-office and virtual work where our employees are empowered to do their best. Generally, employees are expected to work three or more days in-office each week with flexibility depending on their business, client and team needs.
Director, Senior Data Analyst, Office of the Chief Investment Officer (CIO) – SLC Management

The Director, Senior Data Analyst will work in partnership with the Office of the CIO, and the SLC Digital Solutions & Innovations team to identify areas of opportunities where data analysis (including statistical modeling, AI, etc) can be leveraged to create a positive outcome for the asset management

Reporting to the Managing Director, Chief of Staff, Office of the CIO & the Senior Managing Director, Client Experience, Data & Analytics, and Innovation Platforms Owner, the Senior Data Analyst will leverage their in-depth asset management and/or capital markets knowledge to provide a hands-on, strong analytical and data-driven approach to help organize, visualize, and understand large data sets.

We are looking for an individual who thrives in a fast-paced and agile environment and knows how to juggle multiple deadlines. We are looking for a clear communicator who is comfortable negotiating and making recommendations to various levels of leadership.
• Define and clearly articulate problem statements to allow for collaboration on solutions that meet business needs.
<li>Facilitate sessions and drive discussions to uncover critical and/or hidden requirements, the true business need, and associated <span style=""display:inline
Leverage the data assets to provide recommendations and convey key insights to answer business questions. Communicate data insights and findings across the organization via presentations & documents.
Create re-usable data assets for the firm by acquiring, digesting, and analysing raw datasets.
• Create unique and descriptive visualizations that bring data to life, intuitively telling the story behind data.
Work with engineering to develop scalable data pipelines that allow for accurate and insightful views into the data.
<li>Manage discussions, conflicts, and negotiations through to resolution, using communication strategies that align with different audience styles, environments and/or <span style=""display:inline
• Work in collaboration with teams across SLC Management and SLF Corporate
7+ years’ experience in a data analyst role, ideally from asset management industry (CFA preferred).
Expert at writing SQL queries and data analysis with python.
Extensive experience creating visualization using various tools such as PowerBI, and Tableau.
Expert at pulling, cleaning, and manipulating large sets of data into insights.
Have the ability to work independently with your business partners to deliver an outcome.
Understanding of common statistical methods and their real-world applications.
• Experience operating in an ambiguous environment with competing priorities.
• Opportunity to work for a growing global institutional asset manager

• Excellent benefits and wellness programs to support the three pillars of your well-being – mental, physical and financial – including generous vacation and sick time, market-leading paid family, parental and adoption leave, a partially-paid sabbatical program, medical plans, company paid life and AD&D insurance as well as disability programs and more
• Retirement and Stock Purchase programs to help build and enhance your future financial security including a 401(k) plan with an employer-paid match as well as an employer-funded retirement account
• A flexible work environment with a friendly, caring, collaborative and inclusive culture focused on career growth development #WeWinAsATeam
• The opportunity to move along a variety of career paths with amazing networking potential

• Award winning workplace culture - Great Place to Work® Certified in Canada and the U.S., “Best Places to Work in Money Management” by Pension & Investments, “Top 10” employer by the Boston Globe\'s “Top Places to Work” two years running

SLC Management is a regulated business, and this means that there are certain financial reporting and trading preclearance requirements for all employees. Successful candidates will be required to declare personal investment accounts and securities investments as well as seek preclearance on future transactions including those of household members.

We consider various factors in determining actual pay including your skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition to salary, this position is eligible for incentive awards based on individual and business performance as well as a broad range of competitive benefits.

We are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive environment, where all employees feel valued, respected, and supported. We are dedicated to building a diverse workforce offering wide perspectives and creative ideas that benefit our clients, teams and the communities where we operate. Research shows that women and underrepresented groups tend to apply only if they meet 100% of the criteria. We encourage applications from qualified individuals from all backgrounds even if they don’t meet every criteria in the job description.

We will make reasonable accommodations to known physical or mental limitations. Persons with disabilities who need accommodation in the application process or those needing job postings in an alternative format may e-mail a request to
We thank all applicants for showing an interest in this position. Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

column1: 6aff7ede-62e2-424e-9f23-874bb296cd7e,348943675,BMO Financial Group,Risk Specialist Analyst,on_site,"Ontario, Canada",2024-11-04T08:18:02.222Z,"<div class=""css-156jxiv"">

As part of the role, the analyst will provide maned services of vulnerability patch management in Wealth Management. As part of this role, you know Power BI, understand the development life cycle and be able to communicate vulnerabilities with the Senior Leadership team.

Supports the execution of accurate and efficient reporting solutions for risk, regulatory and management information to internal and external stakeholders including regulatory bodies. Supports an efficient and effective risk management function which uses common information sources and practices, reduces ongoing costs, increases service level performance and minimizes risk.
• Monitor, assist and report on patching, vulnerabilities, and configuration management related activities.
• Liaison with 3rd party’s groups, facilitate vulnerabilities exception process, track & coordinate remediation plans and monitor response progress.
• Provide oversight using regular, ad-hoc reports and dashboards to improve prevention.
• Assist with Risk Controls socialization, process awareness and department level audits.
• Supports development and execution of strategic initiatives in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders.
• Acts as a resource for team members with less experience
• Leads or assists with the maintenance, monitoring, measurement & reporting on the status of the risk reporting strategies performance.
• Designs, develops, and implements reporting solutions to meet management and regulatory reporting requirements.
• Provides advice and guidance to assigned business/group on implementation of analytical and reporting solutions.
• Executes work to deliver timely, accurate, and efficient service for scheduled reporting production processes.
• Supports the maintenance, monitoring, and measurement of key risk indicators to internal & external stakeholders.
• Analyses data and information to provide risk-related insights and recommendations for the assigned portfolio e.g. capital at risk modeling, risk/return assessments, etc.
• Supports the interpretation of internal and external policies and regulatory requirements relevant to the analytics and reporting mandate e.g. regulatory reporting.

• Works with various data owners to discover and select available data sources from internal sources and external vendors (e.g. lending system, payment system, external credit rating system) to fulfill analytical needs.
• Ensures strong governance and effective controls across risk reporting activities and information in accordance with enterprise standards.
<li>Analyzes data and highlights significant information including variances, trends, opportunities and exposures
• Provides information and supports the process for internal and external audits.
• Gathers and formats data into regular and ad-hoc reports, and dashboards.
• Supports development and execution of strategic initiatives in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders.
• Supports the maintenance of operational procedures and processes related to analytical and reporting processes.
• Supports the development of tools and delivery of training focused on awareness and understanding of key data elements used in risk reporting.
• Participates in the design, implementation and management of core business processes to ensure the accuracy of risk data used in reporting.
• Organizes work information to ensure accuracy and completeness.
<li>Focus is primarily on business/group within BMO
• Exercises judgment to identify, diagnose, and solve problems within given rules.
• Works independently on a range of complex tasks, which may include unique situations.

• Broader work or accountabilities may be assigned as needed.

    Qualifications:
• Typically between 4 - 6 years of relevant experience and post-secondary degree in related field of study or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
• Knowledge of risk management metrics, KPIs and KRIs
• Experience in vulnerability and configuration management related activities
<li>Technical proficiency gained through education and/or business experience in use of specific tools
• Knowledge of industry trends and regulatory requirements for risk reporting.
• Knowledge of risk management theory, processes and portfolio management reporting techniques.
• Knowledge of reporting & analytics concepts and applications.

8a5def43-6944-480d-a147-1ffb6c2a8c68,348375671,RBC,"Associate Director, Data Insights",on_site,"Toronto, ON, Canada",2024-10-31T08:20:04.825Z,"<div class=""jd-info au-target"">

In the increasingly complex world of financial services, accurate and complete data is critical for risk management and regulatory compliance. Ensuring data integrity begins with an understanding of financial services data, data quality controls and identifying and resolving data observations and issues. As an Associate Director, Data Insights, you will be accountable for driving improvements in Risk and Finance data through the management of identification, root cause analysis, and resolution of data issues for US business critical processes and regulatory reports. Reporting to the Director, you will be responsible in building trust and ensuring the accuracy and completeness of shared data assets and reporting data by managing data quality initiatives.
Data Quality monitoring, observation identification and management
• Review and assessment of exceptions and observations management investigations and reporting, and overseeing and monitoring the remediation of data and processes, as required.

• Managing a team responsible for identifying and investigating data quality observations and issues, performing root cause analysis, and determining steps required to remediate the data and/or business processes.

• Manages and conducts detailed investigations and report findings based on data exceptions, identified observations, and ad hoc requests from critical Report owners and other users of shared data assets.

• Manage a team of analysts that provides monthly summary and detailed data quality reporting to business partners, stakeholders and executive leadership, including data quality exceptions, findings and the status of outstanding observations.

• Regularly review and update data quality process documentation for accuracy and completeness, monitor their efficiency and effectiveness and recommend/implement improvements to controls, investigations, reporting, observations management and work flows.

• Based on root-cause analysis findings, propose process changes/improvements to address gaps or inconsistencies in data, processes and/or controls and raise awareness of best practices and approved provisioning points for commonly used data assets.
• Maintain data quality tests and data quality observation repositories
• Provide leadership and communicate effectively with team members, stakeholders and business partners, resolving conflicts/issues of medium complexity and escalating to others as appropriate.
• 7 + years of experience in financial services and systems including finance and credit risk data, data quality principles, data management, business analysis.
• Knowledge of financial reporting and credit risk business concepts and procedures.
• Intermediate to advanced knowledge of data analysis methods and the ability to analyze and resolve complex data related issues.
• Experience conducting root cause analysis, using tools such as SQL, Excel.
• Experience with business and/or technical documentation and the assessment of existing processes and systems.
• Ability to manage multiple competing priorities in times of stress.
• Experience working with financial services and systems including finance and/or credit risk data
• Experience using Collibra or a similar data management or workflow platform

column1: Participation in the internship program requires that you are located in Canada for the duration of the internship program.

This internship is intended for students in their penultimate academic year, who are pursuing a PhD degree program in Computer Science or a related field. If you are in an earlier academic year we encourage you to apply to the Student Researcher opening at g.co/jobs/PhdSRPCA
Google is a global company and, in order to facilitate efficient collaboration and communication globally, English proficiency is a requirement for this internship program.

To start the application process, you will need an updated CV or resume and a current unofficial or official transcript in English. Click on the “Apply” button on this page and provide the required materials in the appropriate sections (PDFs preferred):
1. In the “Resume Section:” attach an updated CV or resume.
• Please ensure your anticipated graduation date (in MM/YY) is listed on the resume.
2. In the “Education Section:” attach a current or recent unofficial or official transcript in English.

• Under “Degree Status,” select “Now attending” to upload a transcript.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Montreal, QC, Canada
• Currently enrolled in a PhD degree in Computer Science, Linguistics, Statistics, Biostatistics, Applied Mathematics, Operations Research, Economics, Natural Sciences, or related technical field.

• Experience in one area of computer science (e.g., Natural Language Understanding, Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Algorithmic Foundations of Optimization, Quantum Information Science, Data Science, Software Developing, or similar areas).
• Currently attending a degree program in Canada and available to work full time for a minimum of 12 weeks outside of university term time.
• In their penultimate academic year or returning to a degree program after completion of the internship.
• Experience as a researcher, including internships, full-time, or at a lab.
• Experience contributing research communities or efforts, including publishing papers in major conferences or journals.
• Experience with one or more general purpose programming languages (e.g., Python, Java, JavaScript, C/C++, etc.).

Join us for a full-time, 12-14 week paid internship that offers personal and professional development, an executive speaker series, and community-building. Research happens across Google everyday, in many different teams. Our research has already impacted user-facing services across Google, including Search, Maps, and Google Now, and is central to the success of Google Cloud and our planet-scale computing, storage, and networking infrastructure.

Research Interns work closely with Developers to discover, invent, and build at the largest scale. Ideas may come from internal projects. From creating experiments and prototyping implementations to designing architectures, Research Interns work on challenges in artificial intelligence, machine perception, data mining, machine learning, natural language understanding, privacy, computer architecture, networking, operating systems, storage and data management, and more.

Google Research addresses challenges that define the technology of today and tomorrow. From conducting fundamental research to influencing product development, our research teams have the opportunity to impact technology used by billions of people every day.

Our teams aspire to make discoveries that impact everyone, and core to our approach is sharing our research and tools to fuel progress in the field -- we publish regularly in academic journals, release projects as open source, and apply research to Google products.
• Contribute to a productive and innovative team, including working with peers, managers, and partner teams.
Information collected and processed as part of your Google Careers profile, and any job applications you choose to submit is subject to Google\'s Applicant and Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to building a workforce that is representative of the users we serve, creating a culture of belonging, and providing an equal employment opportunity regardless of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), expecting or parents-to-be, criminal histories consistent with legal requirements, or any other basis protected by law. See also Google\'s EEO Policy, Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal, Belonging at Google, and How we hire.
If you have a need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

Google is a global company and, in order to facilitate efficient collaboration and communication globally, English proficiency is a requirement for all roles unless stated otherwise in the job posting.

To all recruitment agencies: Google does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Google employees, or any other organization location. Google is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and experience. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

An industry leading global investor with teams in Toronto to London, New York, Singapore, Sydney and other major cities across North America and Europe. Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you\'re referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

This is an opportunity to join a forward-looking team to progress a digital transformation that will enable OMERS Capital Markets businesses and supporting functions to deliver on our pension promise. Our team is at the early stages of defining a strategic roadmap, and the selected candidate will work with internal partners to understand their needs and deliver outcomes, with excellence. This role joins a team on a journey, with significant future potential in a global, sophisticated, and complex enterprise.

We believe that time together in the office is important for OMERS and Oxford, the strength of our employees, and the work we do for our pension members. Our flexible hybrid work guideline requires teams to come to the office a minimum of 3+ days per week.
Reporting to the Director, Data Integration & Performance Platform, the Senior Business Analyst will support and deliver a variety of change initiatives to mission critical data and investment
column3: >performance platforms. The incumbent will ensure that platforms align to department objectives, and the needs of our internal partners by delivering high value solutions and resolving issues.

column1: • Lead and participate in both Agile and Waterfall projects related to data and performance platform enhancements, system integrations, and process improvements that benefit OMERS investment teams, Corporate Finance and Capital Markets Finance
• Ensure that project scope, objectives, deliverables, and timelines are clearly documented and communicated

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather and analyze requirements for projects impacting the OMERS investment data warehouse, and performance measurement platform (e.g. user stories, business requirements documents, BPMN, data flow diagrams, etc.)
• Design and develop complex investment reporting (SQL, SSRS, OLAP, Power BI etc.)
• Define and execute business test plans, including performance and integration tests
• Ensure change management principles are considered throughout delivery, including the early capture of stakeholder impacts, end user training, and communications
• Partner with technology teams to implement system enhancements, upgrades, and patches
• Act as a subject matter expert for the data warehouse and performance measurement platforms
• Develop and maintain support documentation, including troubleshooting guides and knowledge base articles
• Ensure data accuracy, integrity and availability by monitoring and resolving production data and platform issues, ensuring minimal disruption to investment teams
• Develop and maintain data documentation, including data dictionaries, data lineage, and metadata
• Develop relationships among partners by supporting a culture of transparency, collaboration, and service excellence
• Minimum 6 years experience in business analysis roles supporting data warehouse platforms, including production support, change initiatives and/or large transformation programs
• Expert knowledge of business analysis strategies (requirements elicitation, business process modeling, user stories, source to target mapping etc.)
• Expert knowledge of SQL and relational databases
• Expert knowledge of ETL/ELT tools and design patterns
• Knowledge of investment performance measurement and attribution methodologies for fixed income and equity strategies
• Knowledge of the investment lifecycle and data domains for exchange traded and OTC products, including exotic instruments
• Hands-on experience delivering complex data assets, and a proven ability to manage multiple projects
• Experience implementing and delivering change to leading investment applications (e.g. Eagle, SimCorp, FactSet, Bloomberg)
• Experience with the Azure cloud platform and/or Snowflake are nice to have
• Excellence in all types of communication and facilitation with colleagues at all levels
• Self-motivated with strong, proven, analytical and problem-solving skills
• Thrives in highly collaborative environments with a focus on team success
• Bachelor’s Degree in a related field such as Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Accounting, Finance, and Economics.
• Possesses drive, optimism, and a desire to deliver with excellence and continuously improve
• Experience with using generative AI tools and prompt engineering a nice to have

column1: With our sophisticated tech stack—comprising of industry-leading tools like Databricks, Python, PySpark, and Tableau—we don\'t just analyze data

column3: >At Bell, our purpose is to advance how Canadians connect with each other and the world. We achieve this by providing consumers and businesses with the best network technologies in the world, innovative digital solutions and seamless customer experiences. It’s all developed and delivered by the members of #TeamBell and we’re always on the lookout for people with great skills and experiences.

>We value diversity and provide a supportive, inclusive community where all team members can succeed. And through our commitment to environmental, social and governance initiatives, you will feel good about the greater impact you will have – making every day better for people as they connect, work, learn and play.

>Launch your career at Bell and join the best-in-class Graduate Program. You’ll be empowered to shape your future and inspired to bring your best ideas to work. Throughout the program, you’ll have the chance to connect with mentors and senior leaders who will support your professional and personal growth journey. You’ll develop real-world skills through Bell U, our exclusive learning platform and join other grads attending exclusive events and networking opportunities.
>At Bell, you'll have the opportunity to make a meaningful difference, using your most innovative ideas to bring the best digital connections and experiences to Canadians.
>Benefit from unique opportunities to engage in high-impact projects across our business to develop skills and instincts that will benefit your studies and future career.
>Deliver on transformational projects to launch new product roadmaps, services, and solutions, to enable the customer to do more.
>Expand your professional network with industry leaders, making valuable connections for your future including one-to-one mentorship with experienced leaders.
>Develop your analytical, project management, and communication skills with training and hands-on, real-world experiences to challenge and grow you.
>Interact with our peer-to-peer support network of #TeamBell community and recent grads enrolled in our full-time Graduate program.
>Develop your technical and leadership skills through formal and experiential training programs.
>You will have the opportunity to secure a full-time permanent position in our award-winning Graduate program.

>As an intern, you will support the cutting-edge of Bell’s transformation in critical technologies such as Cloud, AI/ML and Data. You’ll be a part of projects that expose you to the latest software and tools, collaborating with teams across Bell for process improvement and automation. Your day-to-day experience will include opportunities to offer insights that influence business decisions and technology investments across Bell.
>Within our Data Analytics & AI function, we have several opportunities available. For more details on each of the below roles, click here.

>You’re a first to third year student studying towards a university degree OR college/CEGEP diploma in computer science, mathematics, statistics, computer engineering, electrical engineering, or related fields.
>Technical skills (you have some of the following)
>You can analyze, process, and visualize complex data quickly.
>You have experience using SQL for data exploration and insights.
>You have knowledge of data analytics theory, including statistical analysis and quantitative and qualitative modeling.
>You are familiar with programming languages like Python, Scala, R, or similar.
>Some knowledge of analytical tools such as Looker, Kibana, Tableau, and Microstrategy.
>You have knowledge of machine learning and artificial intelligence concepts and their potential applications.
>You are aware of Agile and CI/CD methodologies in software development.
>You are willing to challenge the status quo, experimenting to find solutions that will make a difference in the lives of Bell’s customers.
>You are goal-oriented and can focus on individual tasks while also understanding the bigger picture and how systems work together.
>You are a skilled communicator balancing urgency, empathy and integrity. You speak and write clearly and concisely, and you listen with the purpose of encouraging transparent dialogue.
>You gain the trust of your teams, being known for demonstrating fairness and respect.
>You promote belonging and are flexible, accountable and collaborative.
>You are committed to growing your skills and learning from others.
>Our application process is designed to be straightforward, so you can show us who you are and we can easily spot your #TeamBell potential.

>1. Review our programs and indicate up to three role choices in your application.
2. Complete your video interview within 48 hours.
3. If selected, you will be invited to complete a phone interview or assessment to better understand your skills and experience.
4. If selected from previous step, you will be invited to a virtual panel interview.
>Get started by clicking Apply on this page. Submit a resume, and most recent transcripts (unofficial accepted) to be considered.
>Offers will be made to qualified interns ongoing from September to December 2024 or until all positions are filled.
>Adequate knowledge of French is required for positions in Quebec.

>Additional Information:
Position Type: Management
Job Status: Intern
Job Location: Canada : Ontario : Mississauga || Canada : Nova Scotia : Halifax || Canada : Ontario : Ottawa || Canada : Ontario : Toronto || Canada : Quebec : Montreal
Work Arrangement: Hybrid
Application Deadline: 11/10/2024

>For work arrangements that are ‘Hybrid’, successful candidates must be based in Canada and report to a set Bell office for a minimum of 3 days a week. Recognizing the importance of work-life balance, Bell offers flexibility in work hours based on the business needs.
>Please apply directly online to be considered for this role. Applications through email will not be accepted.

>We know that caring for our team members is at the heart of a healthy, positive and thriving workplace. As part of our team, you’ll enjoy a comprehensive compensation package that includes a competitive salary and a wide range of benefits to support the well-being of you and your family. As soon as you join us, you'll be eligible for medical, dental, vision and mental health benefits that you can tailor to your specific needs. Plus, as a Bell team member, you'll enjoy a 35% discount on our services and access exclusive offers from our partners.

>We value the experiences that have shaped who you are, and we know the diversity of your talent will bring even greater strength to our team. At Bell, everyone belongs and you’ll feel valued, respected and supported as you grow and reach your full potential.

column3: >

>Requisition ID: 210521

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

>Global Banking and Markets (GBM) is a leading Canadian Capital Markets and Investment Banking business with a growing platform in the US and Latin America, operating globally for over 100 years. Scotiabank’s strong U.S. presence provides our clients an important bridge to this key global market for trade and investment flows across the Americas and the world.

>Global Banking & Markets provides a full range of investment banking, credit and risk management products and services relevant to the financing and strategic development needs of our clients. Our products include debt and equity financing, mergers & acquisitions, corporate banking, institutional equity sales, trading and research, fixed income products, derivatives, energy, foreign exchange and precious & metals. We also cross-sell the full range of wholesale products and services offered by the Scotiabank Group.

>Be part of an innovative, Global Capital Markets and Investment Banking business with a unique geographic footprint that puts capital to work for our clients across industries! We work together to drive ambition for every future!
column5: >Global Analytics and Financial Engineering is a critical function within the Bank’s Global Banking and Markets division. The modelers in the department provide the models for pricing and risk management across the business lines. The models developed by the department are used for pricing, hedging, internal and external capital, and trading limits.The associate will be focusing on projects relating to model performance monitoring, computation of initial margin and model governance. Ensures all activities conducted are in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies and procedures.

column1: • Ensure newly developed models or changes to existing models to meet regulatory requirements (e.g., risk sensitivities, residual risk add-ons, etc.) for FRTB capital calculations.
column3: >Assist in conducting and documenting ongoing perfomance monitoring of front-office pricing models.
column5: >Work to streamline ongoing monitoring processes where applicable
column7: >Assist in conducting testing and ongoing monitoring of the initial margin model implemented at the Bank
column9: >Assist in preparing timely responses to requests from regualtory bodies and/or auditors around model performance monitoring and initial margin computation
column11: >Provide subject matter expertise to internal stakeholders regarding ongoing monitoring, intial margin or other regulatory model usage

column3: >Champions a high performance environment and contributes to an inclusive work environment

column1: • Doctorate or Master’s degree in quantitative finance, mathematics, computer science, physics, or other quantitative areas.

column5: >Demonstrable knowledge of numerical methods and computational modelling: for example, finite difference / finite element, Monte Carlo simulation, optimization, artificial intelligence.
column7: >Strong programming skills in C++ or Python and the willingness to learn other programming languages.
column9: >Knowledge of ISDA Standard Initial Margin Model (SIMM) is considered an asset.
column11: >Strong organizational, analytical skills and attention to detail are a must.
column13: >Excellent verbal and written communication and interpersonal skills
column15: >Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

Applies knowledge of analytics algorithms and technologies to develop models, methodologies and supplementary stress testing tools and infrastructures that assess the impact of a stress on risk capital and ensure risks are properly identified, understood and risk related strategies are enabled. Summarizes statistical findings, draws business conclusions, and presents actionable business recommendations used in the management of risks and related activities e.g. defining tolerance for adverse outcomes, capital modelling, assess capital and liquidity, recovery actions, portfolio and risk management strategies and tactics.

column4: >Join our team and what we\'ll accomplish together

column2: >The Data Strategy & Enablement (DSE) team is on a continuous journey to drive TELUS to become a world-class data insights driven organization, delivering world-class data analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI tooling, high quality data, and a data-product/data platform oriented culture. Our team members are passionate, dynamic, entrepreneurial and highly engaged, driven to achieve great outcomes for our customers, our team members, our communities and our environment. We believe in continuous meaningful transformation integrating dynamic change in our professional lives. Our team members are always learning, experimenting and applying tuition value into everything we do.

>
We are searching for a talented candidate to join our team in the role of DataOps Engineer. Reporting to the Manager – Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms, the successful candidate will be responsible for implementing and supporting solutions to enable business product teams to drive value by realizing Analytics and Machine Learning use cases. This role will support our evolving DataOps landscape by providing analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI tooling, improved speed to market, and automation. This role will require the successful candidate to design, develop, and support our evolving strategy, data, and analytics tools as well as provide strong direction for our Cloud based Analytics and Business Intelligence platforms.
>Collaborate with Business and Product teams to enable valuable Analytics and Machine Learning use cases
>Implement DataOps and MLOps processes and automations using the latest tools to effectively operationalize Analytics and AI workflows
>Deploy and manage front-ends and APIs to facilitate teams’ infrastructure needs for Data Analytics and Machine Learning
>Provision infrastructure and secure, reliable data-centric services and applications in GCP for Data & Analytics users across the TELUS enterprise
>Identify opportunities to make improvements to the deployment and support of our DataOps environment demonstrating a DevOps mindset while improving our reliability and speed to market
>Stay up to date of new software, tools, and organizations that are having an impact on the Analytics and Machine Learning landscape
>You are passionate about Agile culture and DevOps practices
>Keen interest in Data Analytics and Machine Learning, and are familiar with the Machine Learning lifecycle
>Experience with the data offerings in Google Cloud Platform (e.g. BigQuery, Dataproc, Dataflow, Vertex AI)
>Experience with front-end development, back-end development, and/or customizing developer portals (e.g. Backstage)
>Experience with Source Version Control (e.g. git), GitOps tooling (e.g. FluxCD, Argo), Kubernetes, and Kustomize
>Hands on experience in Infrastructure-as-Code (e.g. Terraform, Pulumi) and the Python programming language
>Communicate clearly and effectively in both technical and business matters
>Pivot between supporting the urgent needs of the business and working on strategic initiatives
>Experience working on and supporting large, complex technical projects that involve multiple stakeholders and business units
>Undergrad in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Science or equivalent
>5+ years of progressive and relevant work experience with data/analytics platforms, DevOps, machine learning, and/or Developer Portals (e.g. Backstage)

column1: 55e9e768-5d87-45aa-b4be-55d19b301c69,350090244,Roche,Data Product Owner– Finance Data Strategy Team,on_site,"Ontario, Canada",2024-11-11T08:58:02.683Z,"<div class=""jd-info au-target"">

column2: >Roche fosters diversity, equity and inclusion, representing the communities we serve. When dealing with healthcare on a global scale, diversity is an essential ingredient to success. We believe that inclusion is key to understanding people’s varied healthcare needs. Together, we embrace individuality and share a passion for exceptional care. Join Roche, where every voice matters.

column1: We’re advancing science to ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need.

We are seeking a dynamic and experienced Finance Data Product Owner to support our enterprise data product team in Finance. This role will oversee the operations of core enterprise data products in our finance data analytics domain. These products serve as key enablers for decision making in the areas of global workforce, financial costs, personnel cost, sales, research and development financials, and CAPEX. The Data Product Owner will be responsible for driving operations, managing stakeholders, ensuring effective communication with end customers, and coordinating across various teams to enhance the digital system landscape.

You need to be well versed in exploration of data and analytics, and blending this with our core data technologies in our SAP transactional and business warehouse applications and Data Mesh platforms.
You will be collaborating across Finance and beyond to ensure we architect and execute in building a sustainable data foundation for the Finance organization and our business stakeholders.

This role is for a Digital for Finance team member with extensive experience and subject matter expertise in Analytics in Finance in the Pharmaceutical or similar industry, with a comprehensive understanding of key financial processes and systems, organizational needs, and with a value focused approach.
Managing the day-to-day operations of data products, ensuring seamless functionality and continuous improvement
Maintaining data accuracy and compliance with organizational standards
• Define and be accountable for key performance indicators (KPIs) for finance data products to measure their effectiveness and impact
• Applying knowledge to the strategic application within the finance organization and providing insights to enhance decision-making for the organization
Work closely with cross-functional teams, including Informatics, Finance colleagues, and Business stakeholders to define product requirements to enhance the solutions
Act as the primary point of contact for stakeholders, ensuring a prioritized and impactful requirements process is followed and implemented
• Develop a thorough understanding of the digital system landscape, including its components, integrations, and dependencies
• Lead the coordination of technical updates and maintenance activities, ensuring minimal disruption to end users
• Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Finance or related field or equivalent professional experience
• Minimum 5-7 years of experience working with financial systems, workforce data, and related processes
• Proven experience in financial reporting and analysis, including cost management, budgeting, planning, and forecasting
• Strong expertise in managing data products, with an emphasis on operations and data reconciliation
• Knowledge of complex digital systems and an ability to coordinate across multiple teams
• Proven experience in stakeholder management, with exceptional communication and customer interaction skills
Relocation benefits are not available for this position.

column2: >At Roche, more than 100,000 people across 100 countries are pushing back the frontiers of healthcare. Working together, we’ve become one of the world’s leading research-focused healthcare groups. Our success is built on innovation, curiosity and diversity.

>Roche Pharma Canada has its office in Mississauga, Ontario and employs over 850 employees. The Mississauga facility is bright, vibrant, fosters collaboration and teamwork, and is reflective of Roche\'s truly innovative culture.

This requirement is a condition of employment at Roche that applies regardless of whether the position is on a Roche campus or remotely. column1: As the, Senior Manager, Performance Management & Analytics, you lead and oversee performance measurement and analysis in support of a multitude of key stakeholders and executives across the Bank. You are responsible for effective data management including timely delivery of business performance reports and analysis.
Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:

• Develop and automate, in close coordination with IB Retail and IB Analytics, monthly/quarterly management reporting, Executive dashboards, key risk indicators (KRIs) and key performance indicators (KPIs), ensuring alignment to the Operating Model Design requirements and strategic plan, providing relevant reporting/dashboards, insights and action plans to ensure portfolio health.
• Oversee data integrity and analysis of monthly, quarterly and annual IB Insurance reporting, including working with the Retail IB Team responsible for managing operational and administration activities centered around Business Reporting. Seeks opportunities to enhance processes and identifies continuous improvement opportunities to streamline processes through enhancements to policies and procedures and supports the data and reporting requirements for key collateral protection program strategic initiatives and solutions in conjunction with key stakeholders.
• Lead and drive a customer focused culture throughout the team by deepening stakeholder relationships and contributing as a Centre of Expertise for performance analysis and reporting and plays a key liaison role with Strategic Business partners. Leverages process and technology improvements to drive efficiencies with managerial data for effective problem solving and accountability across the team.
• Routinely monitor Performance Management & Sales results utilizing available reporting (e.g. Weekly & Monthly Sales Reports, Counselor/Sales Platform, Scotia Protect, among others)
• Work with Bank’s Insurance Partners in the Region to develop and implement insurance data sharing interfaces to manage effectively relevant Insurance data, identifying and providing requirements to ensure system, process, reporting and program enhancements are developed to improve program effectiveness in a safe manner.
• Develop customer and portfolio insights and management information focused on effectively managing in partnership with key stakeholders i.e. Retail Products, Analytics, Digital Factory, Credit Risk, Business Support and Insurance Partners
• Foster and maintains collaborative relationships with key internal and external stakeholders including senior leaders in the countries to support the achievement of deliverables and overall objectives in addition to maintaining regular communications with internal stakeholders and insurance companies.
• Contribute to the effective development and roll-out of strategic projects by actively participating in cross-functional project teams with departments in Toronto and across IB. Responsible for completing project plan deliverables in a timely manner, representating IB Insurance at regular project team meetings (contributing actively to decisions and communicating deliverables, issues and commitments to the various stakeholders)
• Understand how the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions.
• Create an environment in which his/her team pursues effective and efficient operations of his/her respective areas, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational risk, regulatory compliance risk, AML/ATF risk and conduct risk, including but not limited to responsibilities under the Operational Risk Management Framework, Regulatory Compliance Risk Management Framework, AML/ATF Global Handbook and the Guidelines for Business Conduct.
• Build a high performance environment and implements a people strategy that attracts, retains, develops and motivates their team by fostering an inclusive work environment

column1: Choose a workplace that empowers your impact.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and experience. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

We are a purpose-driven, dynamic and sustainable pension plan. An industry leading global investor with teams in Toronto to London, New York, Singapore, Sydney and other major cities across North America and Europe. We embody the values of our 600,000+ members, placing their best interests at the heart of everything we do.
Join us to accelerate your growth & development, prioritize wellness, build connections, support the communities we live and work.
Don’t just work anywhere — come build tomorrow together with us.
Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you\'re referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

The Risk Management Team’s mandate is to support the achievement of OMERS business objectives by overseeing the actions undertaken by Management to address key investment, pension, governance and legal, operational, people and strategic risks in an integrated and holistic manner. We strive to help OMERS understand and actively decide upon the risks we’re taking in the pursuit of our business objectives.

In support of Risk’s objectives, the Risk Analytics team is focused on providing investment risk analysis support, risk modeling oversight, and end-user data, analytics and risk tools. We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Director of Risk Modeling to lead the risk modeling oversight function. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of risk modeling techniques, a proven track record of navigating complex financial instruments, and the ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels.

We believe that time together in the office is important for OMERS and Oxford, the strength of our employees, and the work we do for our pension members. Our flexible hybrid work guideline requires teams to come to the office a minimum of 4+ days per week.
As a member of this team, you will be responsible for:
• The recommendation, research, oversight, and implementation of all risk modeling inputs used in our Risk System (MSCI Risk Manager), including:
• Standard models for public asset classes such as equities, credit, and bonds.
• Private asset class models (e.g., factor-based, proxy or bespoke approaches) for private credit, real estate, private equity, and infrastructure.
• Oversight of the risk settings used in our risk system to generate scenario outputs and calculate risk metrics such as VaR.
• Effectively interpret and communicate the drivers behind model outputs to stakeholders, ensuring clarity and understanding of risk metrics and their implications.
• Advise on the modeling inputs used in our third-party Asset Liability tool (ORTEC Glass).
• The development of a framework for documentation, calibration, validation and testing that regularly ensures risk models used are appropriate and fit for purpose.

• The maintenance and governance of an inventory of risk modeling and risk settings decisions used to generate analytics for the Risk Management team, ensuring that all risk modeling activities comply with model risk management standards.
• Developing a key relationship between OMERS and our third-party vendors MSCI and ORTEC.
• Providing support and/or guidance for model validation exercises for non-Risk models used across the organization.
• Development of a framework to evaluate modelling options quantitatively (eg. statistical tests) and based on implementation constraints
• Stakeholder communication of our risk modeling choices and recommendations, particularly with the Risk Leadership Team.
• Managing an internal team dedicated to Risk modeling, providing guidance and support to enhance their skills and performance.
To succeed in this role, you have/are:
• Education in Finance, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics or a related field. FRM or CFA designation is an asset.
• At least 7-10 years of experience in a Risk Modeling, Risk Management or Risk-adjacent role

• Deep expertise in modeling financial instruments, both public and private, and a history of success in the design, development, and implementation of advanced risk models to identify, measure, and manager risks associated with investments.
• An ability to work with large datasets and a familiarity with statistical methods
• Familiarity with third-party risk systems. Experience with MSCI’s Risk Manager is an asset.

• Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret, explain, and effectively communicate the key drivers and assumptions behind risk model outputs to both technical and non-technical audiences.

column2: >As one of Canada’s largest defined benefit pension plans, our people-first culture is at its best when our workforce reflects the communities we live and work — and the members we proudly serve.

This sense of belonging and connection is cultivated up, down and across our global organization thanks to our vast network of Employee Resource Groups with executive leader sponsorship, our Purpose@Work committee and employee recognition programs.
